name: Fix Bug #29 — Invisible Montages
description: Diagnose and fix why montages fire but mesh stays in locomotion pose. Root motion, slot config, parent class, animation data verification, NativePostEvaluateAnimation XY shift interaction.
prerequisites: UE5 Editor open with Sworder721 project
ABP_SW_MasterTemplate wired: Locomotion SM -> DefaultSlot -> Output Pose
warnings: Do not modify terrain or EXO files

steps.description: Open ABP_SW_MasterTemplate and verify parent class is SWAnimInstance
steps.detailed_instructions: Open ABP_SW_MasterTemplate in Content Browser by double-clicking it. Click Class Settings button in the toolbar. In the Details panel, check Parent Class. It MUST be SWAnimInstance, NOT UAnimInstance. If it says UAnimInstance, click the Parent Class dropdown and change it to SWAnimInstance. This is critical — SWAnimInstance has NativePostEvaluateAnimation which handles the XY shift guard during montages.
steps.verification: Parent Class shows SWAnimInstance in Class Settings

steps.detailed_instructions: Click on AnimGraph tab. Verify the chain flows left to right: Locomotion State Machine output -> Slot node (DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot) -> Output Pose. If any connection is missing, drag from the output pose pin of the left node to the input pose pin of the right node. The Slot node must show DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot in its details.
steps.verification: Chain visible: Locomotion SM -> Slot (DefaultSlot) -> Output Pose, all connected with white lines

steps.description: Open AM_ComboAttack montage and check its Slot Name
steps.detailed_instructions: In Content Browser, search for AM_ComboAttack and double-click to open it. In the montage editor, look at the timeline at the bottom. You should see a track labeled with a slot name. Click on the Montage Sections area or the Slot Track. In the Details panel or the track header, verify the Slot Name is DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot. If it says something different (like DefaultGroup.UpperBody or blank), right-click the slot track and change it to DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot.
steps.verification: AM_ComboAttack montage Slot track shows DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot

steps.description: Check AM_ComboAttack animation data has actual bone transforms
steps.detailed_instructions: With AM_ComboAttack open, look at the preview viewport. Click the Play button in the preview to play the montage. Watch if the preview skeleton actually moves and punches. If the preview skeleton does NOT move (stays in T-pose or idle), the animation data has near-zero bone deltas from a bad retarget — the animation needs to be re-imported or re-retargeted. If the preview DOES move correctly, the animation data is fine and the problem is elsewhere.
steps.verification: Preview skeleton visually performs a punch animation when played

steps.detailed_instructions: With AM_ComboAttack still open, find the Details panel on the right. Search for Enable Root Motion Translation and set it to FALSE. Search for Enable Root Motion Rotation and set it to FALSE. Search for Force Root Lock and set it to TRUE. Save the montage with Ctrl+S. This prevents the mesh from teleporting when the montage plays.
steps.verification: Root Motion Translation OFF, Root Motion Rotation OFF, Force Root Lock ON

steps.description: Repeat root motion fix for AM_DodgeRoll
steps.detailed_instructions: Open AM_DodgeRoll from Content Browser. In Details: Enable Root Motion Translation = FALSE, Enable Root Motion Rotation = FALSE, Force Root Lock = TRUE. Verify the Slot Name is DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot. Save with Ctrl+S.
steps.verification: AM_DodgeRoll has root motion disabled, force root lock on, correct slot name

steps.description: Repeat root motion fix for AM_Slide
steps.detailed_instructions: Open AM_Slide from Content Browser. In Details: Enable Root Motion Translation = FALSE, Enable Root Motion Rotation = FALSE, Force Root Lock = TRUE. Verify Slot Name = DefaultGroup.DefaultSlot. Save with Ctrl+S.

Open ABP_SW_MasterTemplate. Click Class Settings in toolbar. In Details panel, search for Root Motion Mode. It should be set to Root Motion from Montages Only or No Root Motion Extraction. Do NOT set it to Root Motion from Everything — that causes teleporting. If it says Root Motion from Everything, change it to No Root Motion Extraction.
steps.verification: Root Motion Mode is NOT set to Root Motion from Everything

steps.detailed_instructions: With ABP_SW_MasterTemplate open, press F7 to compile. Verify green checkmark. Press Ctrl+S to save.
steps.verification: Green checkmark on compile, no errors

steps.detailed_instructions: Open BP_SWCharacter from Content Browser. Click on the Mesh component in the Components panel. In Details panel, find Anim Class or Animation Blueprint. It should show ABP_SW_MasterTemplate. If it shows a different AnimBP, click the dropdown and select ABP_SW_MasterTemplate. Compile the BP with F7 and save with Ctrl+S.
steps.verification: BP_SWCharacter Mesh component Anim Class = ABP_SW_MasterTemplate

steps.detailed_instructions: Press Alt+P to start PIE. Wait for the character to spawn. Press left mouse button to punch. Watch the character mesh carefully. The punch animation should play visually on the mesh. If the mesh still stays in idle/locomotion pose, press ShowDebug Animation in the console (backtick key, type ShowDebug Animation, enter) to see what the AnimGraph is doing. Look for the DefaultSlot weight — it should show 1.0 during the punch. Press Escape to stop PIE.
steps.verification: Punch animation visually plays on the character mesh during PIE

steps.description: If montage still invisible — use ShowDebug Animation to diagnose
steps.detailed_instructions: If the punch still does not show visually: Start PIE again. Open console with backtick key. Type: ShowDebug Animation and press Enter. This shows the AnimGraph evaluation overlay. Look for: 1) Active Montages section — should show AM_ComboAttack when punching. 2) Slot weight for DefaultSlot — should be 1.0. 3) If slot weight is 0.0, the Slot node is not connected properly. 4) If slot weight is 1.0 but mesh doesnt move, the animation data has near-zero bone transforms and needs re-retargeting. Take a screenshot of the ShowDebug output for diagnosis.
steps.verification: ShowDebug Animation shows the montage state and slot weights for diagnosis
